subject: Project Management
title: Swimlanes and Classes of Service
description: A short lesson to help you apply Swimlanes and Classes of Service.

hook.text: Not all work is equal. A production outage needs different treatment than a new feature.

buildup.text: Kanban uses 'classes of service' to handle urgent, standard, and fixed-date work differently.

discovery.text: Swimlanes — horizontal rows on the board — visually separate these classes.

twist.text: Without classes of service, urgent work bumps everything. With them, each type flows predictably.

climax.text: Classes of service let teams be responsive without being chaotic.

punchline.text: Not everything is urgent. Treat it that way.

quiz.question: What is the purpose of 'classes of service' in Kanban?
options: To handle different work types with appropriate urgency
To rank team members by skill
To separate management from team boards
